title: Spirit Rover: NASA's Martian Explorer Takes Off
story: Blast off! In 2003, NASA's Spirit Rover rocketed into space, kicking off an exciting journey to the Red Planet. This robotic explorer, launched on June 10, was part of the Mars Exploration Rover mission. Spirit's job? To search for signs of water and past life on Mars. Like a space detective, it would use special tools to study Martian rocks and soil, helping scientists unravel the mysteries of our neighboring planet.

activity_choices: To search for signs of water and past life
activity_answer: To search for signs of water and past life
follow_up_question: What other planets do you think we should explore with rovers?

title: Franklin's Shocking Kite Adventure
story: Zap! Benjamin Franklin flew a kite during a thunderstorm, making a groundbreaking discovery. On June 10, 1752, this curious inventor used a simple kite with a metal key to prove that lightning was a form of electricity. His daring experiment led to the creation of the lightning rod, a device that protects buildings from lightning strikes. Franklin's brave (but dangerous!) act sparked a new understanding of electricity.

title: A Parrot's Potty Mouth Disrupts History
story: Feathers ruffled at The Hermitage in Tennessee on June 10, 1845. As mourners gathered for President Andrew Jackson's funeral, an unexpected guest caused a commotion. title: Bullseye in Space: A Missile Milestone
story: Zoom! A speeding missile raced through the inky blackness of space. But wait - another missile was hot on its tail! In a groundbreaking test on June 10, 1984, the United States successfully shot down a missile with another missile high above Earth's atmosphere. This space-age game of darts marked the first time such an interception happened in the vastness of space, paving the way for new defense technologies.

Planted a flag on an asteroid

title: A Wild Day in Paris
story: Lions and tigers and bears, oh my! In 1793, Paris became home to the world's first public zoo. On June 10, the Ménagerie du Jardin des Plantes opened its gates, inviting curious visitors to see exotic animals up close. This groundbreaking zoo aimed to educate people about wildlife and nature. It started small but grew over time, becoming a beloved attraction for both locals and tourists.

It only had lions and tigers
It was made entirely of cheese
activity_answer: It was the world's first public zoo

title: Mount Rushmore Joins the National Parks

activity_answer: Made it part of the National Park Service

title: Tarawera's Fiery Fury Destroys Natural Wonder
story: The earth rumbled and shook as Tarawera volcano roared to life. On June 10, 1886, this mighty mountain on New Zealand's North Island unleashed its fury. June 10 marked the start of her journey to fame with bands like the Pixies and The Breeders. Deal's guitar skills and unique voice would later help create the hit song 'Cannonball'.
activity_question: What instrument does Kim Deal play besides guitar?
bonus_fact: Kim Deal has an identical twin sister, Kelley, who also became a musician.

title: A Filmmaker's Journey Begins
story: Lights, camera, action! Gina Prince-Bythewood's story started in Los Angeles on June 10, 1969. Born with a creative spark, she grew up to become a talented American director and screenwriter. Her passion for storytelling led her to create powerful films like The Woman King, inspiring audiences around the world.
activity_question: What famous film did Gina Prince-Bythewood direct?
follow_up_question: What other jobs in filmmaking do you think are important besides directing?

title: R&B Star Jo-Jo Hailey's Musical Journey Begins
story: A soulful voice was born in Monroe, North Carolina in 1971. Joel 'Jo-Jo' Hailey entered the world on June 10, destined for musical stardom. He would grow up to become a key member of the popular R&B group Jodeci, known for hits like 'Come And Talk To Me' and 'Cry For You'. Jo-Jo's smooth vocals helped shape the sound of 90s R&B.
activity_question: What R&B group was Jo-Jo Hailey a member of?
bonus_fact: Jo-Jo's birth name is Joel, but he's known by his nickname in the music world.

title: Drumming Up a Storm: Jimmy Chamberlin
story: Bang! Crash! Boom! The rhythmic sounds of drums filled the air as Jimmy Chamberlin entered the world in 1964. Born in Joliet, Illinois on June 10, this future rock star would grow up to become the powerhouse drummer for The Smashing Pumpkins. His beats drove hit songs like '1979' and 'Tonight, Tonight' to the top of the charts.
activity_question: What famous band did Jimmy Chamberlin drum for?
bonus_fact: Drums aren't just for music - they've been used for centuries to communicate over long distances!

title: Hockey Hero Born: Brent Sutter's Icy Journey
story: Skates ready? On June 10, 1962, future hockey star Brent Sutter was born in Viking, Alberta. This Canadian center would go on to win gold medals and play for famous teams like the New York Islanders and Chicago Blackhawks. Later, Sutter even became a coach, leading the New Jersey Devils and Calgary Flames on the ice.
activity_question: What teams did Brent Sutter play for?
activity_choices: New York Islanders and Chicago Blackhawks
Toronto Maple Leafs and Montreal Canadiens
activity_answer: New York Islanders and Chicago Blackhawks
follow_up_question: Can you name another famous Canadian hockey player?
